issue_title: colons in path component are escaped
issue_body:
RFC3986 says pchar includes ":" and "@".
So a url like http://a.example.com/a/b/c/ftp://b.example.com/d/e/f.txt is valid
while furl escapes it and generates http://a.example.com/a/b/c/ftp%3A//b.example.com/d/e/f.txt .

issue_title: Global defaults request/response mock definitions
issue_body:
Persistent mock definition are reused across volatile mock instances via inheritance.

Exposes mock request/response chainable interfaces:
```python
pook.defaults.request.headers({'Client': 'pook/0.1.6'})
pook.defaults.response.headers({'Server': 'pook/0.1.6'})
```

Show RSSI in interpretation when only one block is selected
Support YAML with multiple docs
issue_body:
The YAML standard allows embedding multiple YAML documents into one by separating them with `---` lines.

When `yq` is given these as input, it errors out with:

```
yq: Error while running jq: ComposerError: expected a single document in the stream
  in "<stdin>", line 2, column 1
```

I have a tool that can output yaml or json and when I output json it returns two objects concatenated, in other words:

```
{
   'thing': 1
}
{
  'thing': 2
}
```

If I pass these directly to `jq` it handles them cleanly. It runs the given command on each document.

Because `yq` wraps `jq` it seems like it should be able to handle this in a like manner.

issue_title: Support for non-ascii fake data while maintaining readability for english speakers
issue_body:
**tl;dr;** I'd like to be able to run my test suite with non-ascii test data, while not using characters that most devs in the team can't read and words that most can't pronounce. The objective is to detect encoding errors in the code, while maintaining readability.

In the Bitbucket test suite, we use a [similar library](https://bitbucket.org/bitbucket/fakedata) (to faker) to generate the fake data. At the time I wrote that library, I wasn't aware of faker. One feature of that library I really find useful is its ability to optionally obfuscate strings. So the library can generate strings like "ợɼȧɳɠé", which are quite readable.

I thought it would be cool to add such a feature to faker and then move our test suite to faker (and deprecate fakedata). I'm not sure if you would be open to integrating my changes, given that this is an esoteric feature, but I'd appreciate it if you did.

[This change](https://github.com/chhabrakadabra/faker/commit/7caf0891d470dda51a599a4461b1afa954cbf918) is just an example of the kind of change I'm proposing. If you show interest, I'd be happy to complete the change, add tests and create a pull request.

Thank you for your time.

issue_title: Pickle Error Slots
issue_body:
_From [menyl...@gmail.com](https://code.google.com/u/118237885019602907514/) on April 18, 2011 17:26:25_

What steps will reproduce the problem? 1. Read in a pcap file with IPv4 frames
2. Create ethernet object dpkt.ethernet.Ethernet(eth)
3. Try to pickle it "pickle.dump(eth_cls,file)" What is the expected output? What do you see instead? I expect that the data be pickled. Output is this 

TypeError: a class that defines __slots__ without defining __getstate__ cannot be pickled What version of the product are you using? On what operating system? dpkt version 1.7 on windows Vista with Python 2.6 Please provide any additional information below. I tried removing the __slots__ attribute from the _MetaPacket class in the dpkt module and adding the dynamically attributes to the dictionary directly which did allow me to pickle the class. However this then cause the actual data frame to be stored twice in the __dict__ which increases the size. I am sure they is a better way to accomplish this but I am unfamailar with how meta classes work and didn't want to break anything.

issue_title: Idea: 'fuck' after 'cd <dir_name>' first checks for available directories for misspelt names
issue_body:
When using "cd <dir_name>" in the command line, 'fuck' automatically creates the directory with the exact name specified. This may sometimes cause irritative behaviour if I am trying to open a directory and misspell its name. Though not too serious an issue, **it will surely enhance the usability of the application to predict that I have misspelt an existing directory name.**

This is what is the normal flow of 'fuck':

> jash@Jash:~/mydir$ ls
> dummy
> jash@Jash:~/mydir$ cd dumm
> bash: cd: dumm: No such file or directory
> jash@Jash:~/mydir$ fuck
> mkdir -p dumm && cd dumm
> jash@Jash:~/mydir/dumm$ 

Wouldn't it be great to have fuck predict that I have made a misspelling and suggest opening the right directory for me?

*(PS: I am a student and new to OSS. Be kind enough to forgive me if I make novice mistakes.)*

issue_title: Get recursive group members
issue_body:
I'm trying to use ldap3 to recursively get all users in a group for an authentication project but not having too much luck.

So far I have:

```
ad_server = Server(
    AD_HOST,
    use_ssl=True,
    get_info=ALL
)

connection = Connection(
    ad_server,
    auto_bind=AUTO_BIND_TLS_BEFORE_BIND,
    read_only=True,
    check_names=True,
    user='%s\%s' % (AD_USER_DOMAIN, AD_USER),
    password=AD_PASS,
    return_empty_attributes=True
)

def get_group_users(cn):
    with connection as conn:
        conn.search(
            search_base=cn,
            search_filter='(|(objectCategory=group)(objectCategory=user))',
            search_scope=SUBTREE,
            attributes = ['member', 'objectClass', 'userAccountControl'],
            size_limit=0
        )

        group_data = json.loads(conn.response_to_json())
        attributes = group_data['entries'][0]['attributes']

        for member in attributes['member']:
            get_group_users(member)
        else:
            # Don't want disabled accounts
            if not attributes['userAccountControl'] in [546, 514, 66050, 66082]:
                return group_data['entries'][0]['dn']

        conn.unbind()

print(get_group_users(GROUP_DN))
```

But this isn't quite right, not sure if it's just me being daft, and I can't find any examples of how this could be done. Have you done/seen this done previously that I could pick up some clues from?

issue_title: [Feature Request] Dynamic DNN creation
issue_body:
Currently the DNN interface is very nicely modularized (I like the add_layer<> template, it's clever), but it currently seems impossible to dynamically build a DNN at run time. It looks like you actually aren't too far from this being possible, though. If some runtime-polymorphic version of SUBNET could be created, with a version of add_layer<> that inherits from that SUBNET class, most of the current code could probably support a built-at-run-time network with minimal effort.

Is there a specific reason you chose to restrict network construction to compile-time? It seems to limit your ability to use existing networks that are in formats like .caffemodel

issue_title: How to encode MibTables using pyasn1 codec encoders
issue_body:
Hi!

I am developing code for a NTCIP agent, which is basically SNMP with additional layers. I already have  created the object instances (most part grouped on tables) successfully - but as a requirement I need to encode some of these tables using BER or OER and store in files. Well, the question is:

Considering that pysnmp apparently is built on top of pyasn1 structure, is there a way to (BER) encode a MibTable directly as a sequence? I am stuck trying to do this in an easy way. but it looks like MibTable's are defined as NoneType objects and have no direct links to its rows, so no way for the encoder to know how to navigate through them.

So, I assume the only way to do this is to create manually a SEQUENCE OF object using pyasn1 and add entries by manually navigating through all rows? I mean, for each table I will need to define a pyasn1 structure in order to accomplish this?

Any guidance here will help - I am searching for examples but it looks like no one is interested in encoding tables! I could already encode MibScalarInstance's and it is OK, but when talking about grouping/sequence objects I can't see a way out.

Thank you very much
Luiz Fernando

issue_title: Duplicate entries with different passwords (and format?)
issue_body:
I really don't have a good way of explaining this other than asking you to see the attached image.

I'm using Ubuntu 12.04 and the screenshot shows how I have two different entries, shown by seahorse 3.2.2 with different "formats" (one has icon, the other one doesn't, etc.)

My application code, the one that uses keyring is:

```python
def get_secret(host, user)
    secret = keyring.get_password(host, user)
    
    if secret is None:
        chars = string.ascii_uppercase + string.digits + string.lowercase
        secret = ''.join(random.choice(chars) for _ in range(24))
        fmt = 'Randomly generated secret for %s@%s: %s'
        logging.warn(fmt % (user, host, secret))
            
        keyring.set_password(host, user, secret)
    
    return secret
```

My code doesn't use threads; and something I've seen is that the two entries in the screenshot have different passwords. So, what I get from that is that at least in two different calls to `get_secret` the line `secret = keyring.get_password(host, user)` returns None, which shouldn't be possible (without threads).

Any ideas? Is this a known bug? How can I help you debug and fix this?

issue_title: Different between Facts and Events
issue_body:
First of all, I would like to thank you for your great effort on developing this amazing framework. I am planning to use this in my own project.

I have tried the fraud detection example as below
```python
from durable.lang import *
with ruleset('fraud_detection'):

    @when_all(c.first << m.amount > 100,
              c.second << m.amount > c.first.amount * 2,
              c.third << m.amount > (c.first.amount + c.second.amount) / 2)
    def detected(c): 
        print('fraud detected -> {0} {1}'.format(c.first.amount, c.first.id))
        print('               -> {0} {1}'.format(c.second.amount, c.second.id))
        print('               -> {0} {1}'.format(c.third.amount, c.third.id))

    @when_start
    def start(host):
        sid = 18
        host.post('fraud_detection', {'id': 30, 'sid': sid, 'amount': 120})
        host.post('fraud_detection', {'id': 50, 'sid': sid, 'amount': 1000})

run_all()
```
It works as I expected with the following output
```
fraud detected -> 120 30
               -> 1000 50
               -> 1000 50
```
As far as I know, the difference between facts and events is the life cycle. Each event will only trigger one single action and be retracted automatically after action dispatched, while facts will stay in the same inference set until someone manually retract them. Therefore, I am expecting to see the same result as above even if I assert facts instead of posting events. 

I change the source code to this
```python
from durable.lang import *
with ruleset('fraud_detection'):

    @when_all(c.first << m.amount > 100,
              c.second << m.amount > c.first.amount * 2,
              c.third << m.amount > (c.first.amount + c.second.amount) / 2)
    def detected(c): 
        print('fraud detected -> {0} {1}'.format(c.first.amount, c.first.id))
        print('               -> {0} {1}'.format(c.second.amount, c.second.id))
        print('               -> {0} {1}'.format(c.third.amount, c.third.id))

    @when_start
    def start(host):
        sid = 18
        host.assert_fact('fraud_detection', {'id': 30, 'sid': sid, 'amount': 120})
        host.assert_fact('fraud_detection', {'id': 50, 'sid': sid, 'amount': 1000})

run_all()
```
In this case, there is no output at all. 
Could you please share some light on this case if I misunderstand anything.

request fetch <user>/<repo> must be same as the default gitlab remote
issue_body:
git repo says it supports `user>/<repo>` for the `request fetch` command , like the `request list` command, but it seems to always look for a `gitlab` remote, and the `<request>` id must be on the `gitlab` remote, which means the use of `<user>/<repo>` on the command line is superfluous.

```
git-repo [--path=<path>] [-v...] <target> request <user>/<repo> (list|ls)
git-repo [--path=<path>] [-v...] <target> request <user>/<repo> fetch <request> [-f]
```

```
$ git lab request coala/package_manager list
List of open requests to merge:
 id	title                                                       	URL
 31	MavenRequirement: Prevent test fails                        	https://gitlab.com/coala/dependency_management/merge_requests/31
 30	DistributionRequirementTest: Fix assertRaisesRegex          	https://gitlab.com/coala/dependency_management/merge_requests/30

$ git lab request coala/package_manager fetch 31
Fatal error: Could not find remote gitlab
```

I have a remote for `coala/package_manager` , but it isnt called `gitlab`. (it is my `origin`)

If I do name that remote `gitlab`, then I dont need to specify `coala/package_manager` on the command line, as it is the default anyway, so I can simply use `request fetch 31`.

Rather than removing this unusable feature, I would prefer that it works, at least when there is already a remote for the requested `<user>/<repo>`, but it should also be relatively easy to fetch the MR without a remote for the requested `<user>/<repo>`.

issue_title: before_record_response DOES change real response
issue_body:
### Versions:
python: v3.6
vcrpy: 1.11.1

### Issue:
I tried using the `before_record_response` callback to scrub responses of sensitive auth info before they are written to disc. However, when I have a test that exercises a function that makes 2 requests, the first requesting an auth token, and the second using that auth token, I'm seeing that the actual response from the first request has been mutated.

There was an issue opened previously (https://github.com/kevin1024/vcrpy/issues/291), although a while ago, that asserted the opposite, and @kevin1024 seemed to agree and explain why the response wasn't/shouldn't be changed.

### Repro:
I wrote the following script to reproduce the issue.
```python
import json
import requests
import vcr


VCR = vcr.VCR(
    serializer='yaml',
    cassette_library_dir='cassettes',
    record_mode='once',
)


def filter_json_response_body(fields, replacement='[REDACTED]'):
    """This is a very naive scrubber, for demo purposes.
    """
    def before_record_callback(response):
        string_body = response['body']['string'].decode('utf8')
        body = json.loads(string_body)

        for field in fields:
            if field in body:
                body[field] = replacement

        response['body']['string'] = json.dumps(body).encode()
        return response

    return before_record_callback


with VCR.use_cassette(
    'it_mutates_the_response',
    before_record_response=filter_json_response_body(['text'])
) as cass:

    response = requests.get('http://numbersapi.com/42?json')
    print(response.json())
```

Here is my `requirements.txt`:
```
requests==2.18.4
vcrpy==1.11.1
```

Please let me know if I forgot to include anything!

when using the fortinet connection class the following error occurs:

  File "upload_config.py", line 20, in <module>
    device_type='fortinet', use_keys=False, key_file=None)

- No need to adjust movetime!

issue_title: Add parsing support for organizations, roles, people, etc.

issue_title: Version 1.0?
issue_body:
The version of this library on PyPI is 0.3.5. It's been a long time since the last commit, and with no open issues, that would seem to indicate that the library is very stable.

Also, it seems like libcharsetdetect + related things are all in a settled or stable state as well -- so it doesn't seem like libcharsetdetect needs updating... References: [uchardet-enhanced - 2012](https://bitbucket.org/medoc/uchardet-enhanced), [libcharsetdetect - 2010](https://github.com/batterseapower/libcharsetdetect), [Mozilla universalchardet - 2012](http://mxr.mozilla.org/seamonkey/source/extensions/universalchardet/)...

What is missing, to take this library to "1.0"? Is there, perhaps, a list of improvements that could be contributed?

issue_body:
Hi,
I posted this originally on the jedi-vim issue list (https://github.com/davidhalter/jedi-vim/issues/438) but I was told that this is jedi issue so I moved it here.

Jedi is not completing slightly complex objects as "x" in the following example:

```python
import pandas as pd
d = {'one' : pd.Series([1., 2., 3.], index=['a', 'b', 'c']), 'two' : pd.Series([1., 2., 3., 4.], index=['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'])}
df = pd.DataFrame(d)
x = df.head(2)
x.        # no completion on this data-frame
```
E.g. in Vim, the command line shows: "=jedi#complete_opened(0) Omni completion (^O^N^P) Pattern not found"

Another example with numpy: 
```python
ll = [i for i in range(0,int(30e3))]
nn = np.array(ll)   
nn.   # no completion
```
Could anybody please help out on this or does anybody have suggestions what could go wrong? It would be great if this issue could be fixed as it would make editors as vim really nice and competitive to program python in.

Many thanks, David

issue_title: Wrong completion for arbitrary argument lists
issue_body:
Accroding to [PEP-484](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0484/#arbitrary-argument-lists-and-default-argument-values), for the following function declaration
```python
def foo(*args: str, **kwds: int):
```
> In the body of function foo, the type of variable args is deduced as Tuple[str, ...] and the type of variable kwds is Dict[str, int].

I am afraid jedi suggests completions for `args` as if its type were `str`:
![args](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2445711/32145388-f84eb90a-bcc7-11e7-8a4e-caa8c076c566.png)
 and for `kwds` as if its type were `int`:
![kwds](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2445711/32145390-f8766a40-bcc7-11e7-96e9-9f538136fc49.png)

The examples are from my `IPython 6.2.1` running `Python 3.6.3` and `jedi==0.11.0`. I observed this behavior when I was using the `deoplete-jedi` plugin in NeoVim, but I just wanted to provide a simpler reproduction.

issue_title: Will not mount on boot from fstab
issue_body:
Drive will not mount on boot. The entry in `/etc/fstab` is the following:

    /var/cache/gdfs.creds /mnt/Google-Drive gdfs    allow_other     0       0 

My other disks mount correctly and strangely, if I drop to a shell when the error occurs at boot and use `mount -a`, it will mount correctly.

I'm using I am using Ubuntu 14.04.3 LTS with kernel 3.13.0-62-generic.

issue_title: Move to template files for colors
issue_body:
Right now `wal` generates output files by looping over the colors and formatting them. This works for the simple output files (`colors.css`, `colors.sh` etc) but becomes overly complicated when working with more complex file formats (`xcolors` (xrdb)).

What I'm thinking is that we move to template files instead. These files would be identical to the current outputted files but full of markers telling `wal` where to put each color. This way we can also have comments in the files telling the user `wal` generated them as well as proper alignment etc.

issue_title: Recursive -i argument
issue_body:
Quick idea i just wanted to use, but saw it didn't work:

using argument `-i <my-path>` where the directory <my-path> itself doesn't contain any pictures, but the subdirectories of it do.
In this folder I store multiple sets of wallpapers and i'd love to see pywal choosing a random one by having `-i` searching for images recursively.

issue_title: Installation on Python 3.7 raises ValueError: bad marshal data (unknown type code)
issue_body:
When installing on Python "nightly" on Travis-CI, I see this error:

    ValueError: bad marshal data (unknown type code)

For the full output see https://travis-ci.org/bastibe/SoundFile/jobs/365467380.

I searched a bit for the error message, and the recommended solutions suggest to remove `.pyc` files.

Since the `pycparser` tarball contains `.pyc` files, I guess the error has something to do with them.

But I really don't know, it might be something completely different.

Probably related: #135

issue_title: Report merged reads and writes per disk
issue_body:
I have spinning disks and during raid-10 rebuild I noticed this:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/89186/12823286/0e51f90e-cb63-11e5-832e-64f92bc7465f.png)

With a theoretical maximum of ~200 IOPS having 2000+ IOPS looks a bit surreal. I dug a bit and found out that these are merged reads and the do not mean much for monitoring, since you don't measure how hard you hit your disks.

I suggest reporting merged reads and writes:

* https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/ABI/testing/procfs-diskstats

It also looks like time spent in IO for spinning disks could be more than 1 second per 1 second of real time. This also looks like a science fiction, you can't have one disk head in many locations at once, that's witchcraft^W quantum computing.

I suggest also reporting 14th column from the document above, that seems to hold the right metric.

issue_title: cpu_count() should guard against return value < 1 and return None
issue_body: #1078 made me think about this. Basically we make the promise that if the number of CPUs cannot be determined `psutil.cpu_count()` will return None. Anyway, we should guard against possible incorrect values returned by the kernel (e.g. -1 or 0) and also return None.

issue_title: Ensure that stream management is enabled synchronously
issue_body:
Apparently, it is currently possible to make aioxmpp fail by sending a ``<{sm}r/>`` right after enabling stream management. Of course, this is nonsensical to do normally, but in combination with #173, it is possible that we’re sending stanzas right after the ``<{sm}enable/>``, prompting the peer to reply with ``<{sm}enabled/>``, some replies and then ``<{sm}r/>``. 

In this case, it is apparently possible (I’ve got logs to prove it!) that the SM handlers are not attached to the XML stream yet, thus making the stream fail with ``unsupported-stanza-type``.

issue_title: Rework template variables
issue_body:
Consider the following variable (yaml) and template files:

```yml
---
foo: 1
bar: 2
baz: 3
```

```jinja2
foo * bar + baz = {{ foo * bar + baz }}
```

Wouldn't it be nice to be able to override the variables from command-line call. For example, the following command-line call introduces the inline variables and will return `foo * bar + baz = 9`:

```bash
yasha -V defaults.yml -v foo 2 -v bar 3 template.j2
```

This change would change the behavior of `-v --variables` option. The capital `-V` would be now used for variable files and -v for inline variables.

```python
@click.option("-V", type=click.File("rb"), multiple=True)
@click.option("-v", type=(str, str), multiple=True)
```

issue_title: Python 3 - 503 Error: send HELO first
issue_body:
On Python 3 I've got the following error while trying to send any mail: `503 Error: send HELO first`.

I managed to investigate the source and it appears to be as follows.

You subclass `smtpd.SMTPChannel` and override the method `smtp_EHLO`.
In the CPython implementation of this method the instance variable `self.seen_greeting` is set and then, in the `smtp_RCPT` method, there is a check on that same variable ([relevant code here](https://hg.python.org/cpython/file/3.4/Lib/smtpd.py#l501)).
This check fails and trigger the error.

As far as I can see, there is no need to override the `smtp_EHLO` method.

issue_title: client_async.__del__ can throw exception on failed construction.
issue_body: In `client_async.py` there is a line `os.close(self._wake_r)`. Unfortunately as that property is only set at the end of `__init__` it is likely that property will not exist if there is an exception raised from within `__init__` during construction. This line needs to be guarded with a `if hasattr(self, '_wake_r')` or similiar to avoid creating a second exception that obscures the exception being raised by `__init__`

issue_title: Adapt to lensfun API backwards incompatible changes
issue_body:
The next lensfun version (after 0.3.2) has some backwards incompatible changes and other things that should be adapted in lensfunpy: https://sourceforge.net/p/lensfun/code/ci/master/tree/ChangeLog

As lensfunpy exposes some of the breaking functions (`Lens.interpolate_*`) it will be backwards incompatible as well, meaning the next version will be 2.0.0. For people building lensfunpy from source, this would also raise the minimum supported lensfun version to the next released lensfun version.

The functions with breaking API in lensfunpy are more advanced and probably not used in the majority of cases, which is good.

Given that this is more work than usual and the next lensfun version is still in development and may introduce more breaking changes, I estimate that it will be a good while until the next lensfunpy version.

issue_title: Distrusted Symantec CAs
issue_body:
Hello,

I set out to batch-check if our customers are affected by the upcoming changes in Google Chrome and Firefox regarding Symantec's certificates (https://blog.chromium.org/2018/02/a-secure-web-is-here-to-stay.html). To my surprise, I'm having quite a hard time doing that. 

As far as I understand:
- The average webmaster maybe does not necessarily know which brands belong to Symantec (Thawte, RapidSSL and GeoTrust).
- Right at the moment, the affected certificates are still in the trust stores. So obviously, sslyze will tell me that my certificate is OK, even when in some weeks my website will be effectively down for certain browsers.

I guess many websites will miss a lot of users on the day when this hits. So I'm wondering how could I check for this issue in advance?

As far as I understand: If I had a trust store that already reflects the upcoming changes in Chrome / Firefox I could use that with sslyze. For example, would it be possible to filter the affected cerfificates out when doing a "--update_trust_stores"? 

For example, Google Chrome seems to have a hardcoded list in the source to issue warnings in the browser console, but I have no clue how to make this usable.

issue_title: Add global attribute in {{mustache}}
issue_body: Glances allows you to define action on trigger (see: http://glances.readthedocs.org/en/latest/glances-doc.html#actions). Inside the action's command line, it is possible to use {{mustache}} where mustache is one of the attribute of the current plugin section. It should be great to allow user to define external global attribute like: hostname... (TODO: define other attributes).

issue_title: Make plugins disable and export CLI option dynamical
issue_body:
#### Description

In Glances < 3.0, if you want to disable one or more plugin you should enter the following command line:

     glances --disable-network --disable-load

The current enhancement request proposes replace it by:

    glances --disable-plugin network,load

The new syntax will bring some dynamic function for the developer. When a new plugin is added, no more need to declare it in the main.py script.

Same kind of call should be done for the export module. 

A new --modules-list option should be available to display the plugins and exporters list:

```
$ glances --modules-list
Plugins list: load, docker, help, ip, memswap, processlist, cloud, uptime, network, percpu, irq, system, diskio, gpu, folders, core, fs, raid, mem, alert, psutilversion, hddtemp, sensors, now, quicklook, wifi, cpu, processcount, amps, batpercent, ports
Exporters list: statsd, riemann, couchdb, prometheus, rabbitmq, kafka, json, elasticsearch, influxdb, opentsdb, zeromq, csv, restful, cassandra
```

#### Versions

* Glances (glances -V): Target release 3.0

#### Notes

Break the command line compatibility.

issue_title: Derive languages from XML
issue_body:
Goal: various languages are detected from XML.

* Ant: `<project>` with `name`.
* Maven: `<project>` with `xmlns` matching `http://maven.apache.org/POM/*`, see e.g. https://maven.apache.org/guides/getting-started/maven-in-five-minutes.html.
* EJB XML: has `xmlns="http://xmlns.jcp.org/xml/ns/javaee"`, see also http://www.oracle.com/webfolder/technetwork/jsc/xml/ns/javaee/index.html
* DocBook: There are multiple ways, see http://docbook.org/tdg5/en/html/ch02.html#ch02-makexml.
  1. first elements has `xlmns` of `http://docbook.org/ns/docbook`
  1. `<book><title>`  or `<chapter><title>`
  1.  DTD is something like `<!DOCTYPE book PUBLIC "-//OASIS//DTD DocBook V5.0//EN" "http://www.oasis-open.org/docbook/xml/5.0/docbook.dtd">`

issue_title: Add function to download artifacts
issue_body:
per [doc](https://circleci.com/docs/api/v1-reference/#download-artifact) you can download an artifact of a file. 

This should be a method of the API. 

We should also write a private `_download()` helper that takes the url, destination directory, and filename as parameters. 

Something like this should work: 

```
    def _download(self, url, destdir=None, filename=None):
        """File download helper

        Args:
            url (str):
                Url to file
            destdir (str):
                Optional full path to the destination directory

                defaults to $PWD
            filename (str):
                Optional file name

                defaults to the end of the URL
        """
        if not filename:
            filename = url.split('/')[-1]

        if not destdir:
            destdir = os.getcwd()

        endpoint = "{0}?circle-token={1}".format(url, self.token)

        resp = requests.get(endpoint, stream=True)

        path = '{0}/{1}'.format(destdir, filename)
        with open(path, 'wb') as f:
            for chunk in resp.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                if chunk:
                    f.write(chunk)

        return filename
```

issue_title: Login redirects to the /home URL, causes confusion
issue_body:
EDIT: Changing issue description, since I can't just delete the whole thing.

I just ran into a rather frustrating issue (that is mostly my fault).

Here's the scenario:

- app is in `debug` mode
- had dummy `User` instances already loaded in the db from other dev work
    - not using `UserMixin` because I haven't in prior projects and don't really like all that extra code
- wired up flask-user as i've done before in other projects
- use forgot-password to receive email & set password on one of the fake users
- totally forget that there's an `active` flag on the `User`
- stay in endless loop that looks like login is not working & constantly redirecting to itself, **but to the new /home URL**, leading someone who has used flask-user in the past to wonder where the heck that came from (and then discover it's hard-coded in the pkg).

I just wasted hours trying to figure out where in the heck this `/home` URL came from, what view it was tied to, and why my once-working Flask-User process was suddenly broken in the latest version. And it was all because I hadn't set any `active` flags to `True`. Oh, and once I figured that out, now I **have** to have a `has_confirmed_email` attr/method on my `User` object.

*Sigh.*

**Suggestion:** if the app is in `debug` mode, it'd be pretty awesome to have some more errors thrown, console messages logged out, or *something* that helps identify why things are not working in places they *should*, but something that is expected is either not present, or not what it should be. i know there are some calls to set flash messages, but that's not enough in all scenarios--especially if we're customizing templates and don't have flash messages included. 

Maybe it's just me, but Flask-User is *increasing* the amount of schema & model attributes it needs just to successfully pull off **authentication**. This is starting to feel like very poor design spiraling out of control. I'm not sure I am going to want to keep using Flask-User if this is how the project is going to be going in the future. I've now done two upgrades, and both times have found me wasting hours trying to discover unintuitive behavior based on schema/model assumptions.

**Above all else**, if the app is in `debug`, that should be the time to throw all kinds of show-stopping messages at a developer that they don't have things right. Or maybe allow it to be activated with a flag or something. But when you're using a 3rd-party lib that you're trying to treat like a black box, when the box doesn't work and doesn't tell you, things get pretty annoying rather quickly.

issue_title: Linsear Write misses words that are 3 syllables long
issue_body:
In the `linsear_write_formula` function, if a word has 3 syllables it won't be counted towards the score.

```python
    if self.syllable_count(value) < 3:
        easy_word.append(value)
    elif self.syllable_count(value) > 3:
        difficult_word.append(value)
```

From [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linsear_Write):

> 1. Find a 100-word sample from your writing.
> 2. Calculate the **easy words (defined as two syllables or less)** and place a number "1" over each word, even including a, an, the, and other simple words.
> 3. Calculate the **hard words (defined as three syllables or more)** and place a number "3" over each word as pronounced by the dictionary.
> 4. Multiply the number of easy words times "1."
> 5. Multiply the number of hard words times "3."
> 6. Add the two previous numbers together.
> 7. Divide that total by the number of sentences.
> 8. If your answer is >20, divide by "2," and that is your answer.
> 9. If your answer is <20 or equal to 20, subtract "2," and then divide by "2." That is your answer.

issue_title: What's the benefit for aiohttp-wsgi + django?
issue_body:
It seems that `aiohttp-wsgi` just runs an application in an executor, which by default [is just a thread pool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-eventloop.html#executor), with a default pool size of 5 threads.

So if the application is designed with blocking APIs, such as flask and django, it will actually be executed via multi-threading and could not take advantage of co-routines. Then what's the benefit for using `aiohttp-wsgi`?

issue_title: Issue with Center Across Property
issue_body:
Hi John,

First I'd like to thank you for a wonderful package. It's making my life much easier! I have what I think is a small bug, but it might also be an issue with the documentation:

Setting the `center_across` property in a cell format causes an Exception because xlsxwriter tries to convert the property into the `set_center_across` method and fails as `set_center_across` doesn't accept any arguments. Judging from commit `32db5c0`, there was a fix to the method, but that broke the way of setting it as a property. The documentation I'm referring to is here: [http://xlsxwriter.readthedocs.io/format.html](http://xlsxwriter.readthedocs.io/format.html) under "Format methods and Format properties".

From what I can tell, you may only be allowed to set center across through the `align` property as of the current version or something like that.

I am using Python version 3.5.2 and XlsxWriter 0.9.3.

Here is some code that demonstrates the problem:

```python

import xlsxwriter

workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook('hello.xlsx')
worksheet = workbook.add_worksheet()

format = workbook.add_format({"center_across": True})

worksheet.write('A1', 'Hello world', cell_format = format)

workbook.close()

issue_title: No support for eventlet
issue_body:
This is just knowledge sharing. 
I think that python-etcd3 is blocking eventlet greenthreads.
Here's a blocking example:
```
# python-etcd3 == 0.8.0
# eventlet == 0.23.0
import etcd3
import eventlet
eventlet.monkey_patch()


def thread1():
    client = etcd3.client(host='127.0.0.1', port=2379)
    events_iterator, cancel = client.watch("/foo")
    for event in events_iterator:
        print(event)


def thread2():
    i = 0
    while True:
        print(i)
        eventlet.sleep(1)
        i += 1


eventlet.spawn_n(thread1)
eventlet.spawn_n(thread2)

while True:
    eventlet.sleep(1)

```

The problem is probably in grpcio lib as it stops here:
```
File "src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/channel.pyx.pxi", line 432, in grpc._cython.cygrpc.Channel.next_call_event

```

If you have any information about this topic I will appreciate.

issue_title: login.py中在线检测start_receiving的一个BUG
issue_body:
首先感觉楼主提供了这么好的一个库。
在使用过程中发现，在保持手机客户一直在线的情况下，挂机总是超不过24小时。从终端显示上看，是经过了5个connect error后就提示LOG OUT。但是这5个connect error不是连续的出现的，总时间大概有20多分钟。也就是说在网络不稳定的情况下，即使网络恢复了，错误的次数还是累计的。经过检查发现，在login.py的start_receiving函数中，当sync_check返回0时，没有重置错误计数器retryCount。而且直接continue了
```python
                i = sync_check(self)
                if i is None:
                    **self.alive = False
                elif i == '0':**
                    continue
```
于是手工在continue前加上  retryCount  = 0 后挂机，截止目前正常。

issue_title: Support for Doxygen image size
issue_body: Hi, and thank you for this awesome extension! I have a small issue related to image sizing (it's probably more of a feature suggestion, but I'm submitting it anyway). In Doxygen you can use the following syntax to specify image size : `\image html test.png width=10px`. In the XML output, this translates to : `width=10px`. The issue is that the size information is lost during Sphinx HTML export, resulting in something like ``. I have also tried an alternate approach : in Doxygen, I used `\xmlonly \endxmlonly` which simply results in `` in Doxygen output. However, neither the `style` nor the `width` tags are take into account by Breathe/Sphinx when generating the HTML files. I don't know if this is a Sphinx limitation, but it could be a nice feature to somehow retrieve the image size to make documentation a little more appealing ;)

issue_title: Doubly invoking Engine.process.wait_for_return_code()
issue_body:
I was reading the source for uci.py, and was puzzled by the fact that (using PopenProcess, not sure about MockProcess or SpurProcess), the Engine.on_terminated is only called after the block:

```
        # this is in PopenProcess._receiving_thread_target
        if self.is_alive():
            self.terminate()
            self.wait_for_return_code()
```

But then Engine.on_terminated will first thing itself call `self.process.wait_for_return_code()`. I see that it's not a problem to call `wait_for_return_code` twice on a Popen object. I was just wondering about the logic of this. Is there some specific reason to call it both at the end of the Popen thread main loop, and also in the Engine `on_terminated` handler? Or is this just an accident of how the code developed, that doesn't hurt anything the way it is? (Or perhaps it's relevant to the other two process thread objects, that I'm ignoring.)

This is not a bug, I was just curious, and didn't know where to ask.

issue_title: Validation for tuples?
issue_body:
I would like to define a schema for a tuple: 

```
Schema( ( Any( int, 'foo', 'bar') ) )( ('foo', 'bar) )
```

I found that voluptuous does not understand the tuple information. For this I had to trick it using: 

```
Schema( All(tuple, Coerce(list), [ Any(int, 'foo', 'bar') ]))
```

But this time I get this error:

```
/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/voluptuous/schema_builder.pyc in validate_mapping(path, iterable, out)
    270                         # include if it's not marked for removal
    271                         if not is_remove:
--> 272                             out[new_key] = cval
    273                         else:
    274                             remove_key = True

TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'
```

So I eventually wrote this workaround: 

```
Schema( All(tuple, Coerce(list), [ Any(int, 'foo', 'bar') ], Coerce(tuple)))
```

issue_title: Travis Failing Test Cases in Master
Can voluptuous play well with pyparsing?
issue_body:
I am wondering how good it could be to have a native support for `pyparsing` in `voluptuous`. They are both some really powerful packages and I have the intuition they could play well together. 

Isn't that schema nice?

```
s = Schema( Suppress(':') + Word('abc') + Suppress(':') )
s(':aabbcc:')
aabbcc   
```

A first step could be easily achieved by making a pyparsing `ParserElement` callable and then retrieve its first element: 

```
class PyparsingInvalid(Invalid):
    pass

class Pyparsing(object):
    def __call__(self, value):
        try:
            return self.pyparsing_schema.parseString(value)[0]
        except ParseException as e:
            raise PyparsingInvalid(e.msg)
    def __init__(self, schema):
        self.pyparsing_schema = stringStart + schema + stringEnd
```

The minor difference with the initial idea is the need to explicitly cast a `ParseElement` to a `Schema`:
 
```
s = Schema( Pyparsing(Suppress(':') + Word('abc') + Suppress(':')) )
```

In order to enhance the synergy between `pyparsing` and `voluptuous` it would be good to make both packages *more* compatible. This could be done on both sides: 

1. pyparsing `ParseElement`

Making `__call__` not set the default result name, but instead call `parseString` which will result in an unwanted compatibility break. 

2. voluptuous

Detect if the input is a `pyparsing.ParserElement` and automatically wrap the `Pyparsing` class around it. 

```
try: 
    from pyparsing import ParserElement as _parserElement
except ImportError: 
    class _parserElement(): 
       pass
...

def wrap_pyparsing(element):
    if isinstance(element, _parseElement):
        return Pyparsing(element)
    return element
```

One last point to consider is to define what should `Pyparsing` return once called: 

1. An instance of `ParseResults` which should not be very useful in a voluptuous schema
2. The first element: `foo.parseString(bar)[0]`
3. A `list` or a `dict` with `list(foo.parseString(bar)[0])`
4. Something configurable by the user using either a `kwargs` or a global setting. 

Regarding this latter point, the most straightforward way to increase the synergy would be to keep the proposed `Pyparsing` and add to it the ability to choose what output the user wants: 

```
Schema(Pyparsing(Suppress(':') + Word('abc') + Suppress(':'), output=lambda x: x[0]))
```

Or...

```
Schema(All(
   Pyparsing(Suppress(':') + Word('abc') + Suppress(':')),
   lambda x: x[0]
))
```



Any better idea?

issue_title: Caching the results to reduce response time and calls to nseindia.com
waittime behavior
issue_body:
Thanks for all of the effort you've put into this library. It's the only one that I've found to be actively developed and work well.

There is a bug with respect to either the logic or exception message for waittime in the PingTransmitter class:

```
       if self.waittime <= 0:
            raise ValueError(
                "wait time must be greater than or equal to zero")
```

When `waittime` is set to `0`, the exception is raised, which states that the 

> wait time must be greater than or equal to zero

According to the exception, `0` should be a valid value, but it isn't

More to the point, what prompted me to start digging into this is that I was looking for a way to exclude the use of the `-w` flag on Linux altogether. I want ping to return as soon as it's done.

As a feature request in addition to the bug above, it would be very helpful to be able to set waittime to `0` and have the `-w` flag not be passed to ping.

issue_title: Provide a memory-backed implementation of IWormhole
issue_body:
For testing applications built on magic-wormhole, it would be convenient to have a Python object that pretended to be a magic-wormhole server and supported all of the normal wormhole interactions purely via in-memory interactions with Python objects.

The point of such a thing would not be to engage in the same cryptographic exchanges as occur for real-world magic-wormhole usage (though it wouldn't necessarily be bad if it _did_).  Rather, the point would be to provide equivalent functionality at the level of Python object interactions.  Primarily, if some Python code calls `send` and some other Python code calls `receive` and they each pass the right values, they should be able to pass some data between them.

This would remove the need for applications that use magic-wormhole to run a real wormhole server themselves (something that is complicated, slow, error prone, and -- due to the cryptography it performed by a real server -- presents primarily very obscure failure modes) in order to have automated test coverage for their own logic.

issue_title: fix timing renderer (JS is broken)
issue_body:
If you run `wormhole --dump-timing=tx.json send` on one side, and `wormhole --dump-timing=rx.json receive` on the other, then copy both files to the same place, you can run `cd misc && python dump-timing.py tx.json rx.json` and it will open a web browser and show you a d3.js-based zoomable/pan-able timeline of the messages exchanged. It's pretty handy for finding places to squeeze out roundtrips.

But I broke it when I did the massive 0.8.0 refactoring. The task is to fix the javascript (in `misc/web/timeline.js`) to make it work once more.

issue_title: Incorrect metadata causing image to fetch not squashed layers into image
issue_body:
```bash
$ docker --version
Docker version 1.9.1, build ee06d03/1.9.1
$ docker pull docker.io/jboss/wildfly:latest
...
$ docker history docker.io/jboss/wildfly:latest
... 21 layers listed
$ docker-squash -t jboss/wildfly:squashed docker.io/jboss/wildfly:latest
... Squashing 21 layers
$ docker history jboss/wildfly:squashed
.. 21 layers listed
```

By providing `-f 20`, the most recent 20 layers are squashed into one resulting in a total of 2 layers, as expected. However, using `-f 21` results in no layers being squashed at all.

1. Is it possible to squash all layers into a single layer? Or is two layers the minimum?
2. This seems to have been introduced by #59 . When from_layer is None, it uses the length of layers. Maybe this should be length of layers - 1 ?

CC @twaugh

issue_body:
Hello! Can anyone explain to me how to upgrade database entries in parallel? I have a db file opened in one process that needs to iterate over all entries while updating with a certain key (different values) if not already existent. I need a few hundred concurrent threads.

```
#!/usr/bin/python3

from unqlite import UnQLite
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor

db = UnQLite('tmp.db')
coll = db.collection('coll')
coll.create()

def fill_db():
    global coll
    for i in range(10):
        s = dict()
        s['id'] = i
        s['flag'] = True
        coll.store(s)

def show_db():
    global coll
    for i in range(len(coll)):
        s = coll.fetch(i)
        print('entry', i, '=>', s)

def upd(idx):
    global coll
    print('Working on', idx)
    c = coll.fetch(idx)
    c['other'] = 1
    if not c.update(idx, c):
        print('Error during update', idx)
    else:
        coll.commit()

def update_db():
    global coll

    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as pool:
        for i in range(len(coll)):
            if 'other' in coll[i].keys():
                continue
            
            # update each item if key 'other' is not yet present
            pool.submit(upd, i)

        print('Done!')
        pool.shutdown(wait=True)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    fill_db()
    show_db()
    update_db()
    show_db()

    db.close()
```

Python 3.6.5

issue_title: Methods using async_contextmanager decorator don't pass pylint
issue_body:
I have a context manager method using the `async_contextmanager` decorator:

```
@asyncio_extras.async_contextmanager
async def scoped_session():
    session = Session()
    try:
        yield session
        session.commit()
    except:
        session.rollback()
        raise
    finally:
        session.close()
```

And then I use this context manager like so:

```
async def create_api_key(module):
    async with scoped_session() as session:
        pass
    pass
```

When I try to run this through `pylint`, it fails, being unable to find the context manager object's `__aenter__` and `__aexit__` methods.

```
$ pylint -E file.py
No config file found, using default configuration
************* Module file
E:  6,10: Async context manager 'generator' doesn't implement __aenter__ and __aexit__. (not-async-context-manager)
```

issue_title: Kerberos support
Follow Up Flags
issue_body:
Hi. I use Office365 and I use the follow up flags and categories a lot. 

Since the Exchange mail rules are not that helpful I want to use your library to:
- set flags (with start and end date)
- set categories
- mark the message as read
- move the message to a subfolder

I guess the filtering of mails should not be a problem. Your README seems rather informative on that subject. And since my script would run a lot I could probably filter to only the last N minutes which should speed up the filtering.

To my actual question:
Is there a way to set the flags? With start and due date. Also: in my App on Android (Nine folders) I can change the subject of these "Tasks" 
I guess it should be possible to modify the subject of one flagged email and look for changes to reproduce this behaviour. However I have not found any hint that an email is indeed flagged. They definitely don't show up in the tasks. At lest not the last time I looked.

I hope this makes sense.
Happy new year and keep up the good work.

Best regards
Roland

issue_title: s3boto3 en/decoding issue
issue_body:
I recently started testing the new s3boto3 backend, and stumbled upon some unicode/str mixups (I'm still rolling with python2.7).

I have a file field with a path containing non ASCII characters and I encountered an issue at file loading ([here](https://github.com/jschneier/django-storages/blob/1.5.0/storages/backends/s3boto3.py#L94)). After further investigation, it seems that boto3 expects each ```name``` argument (the one given to each resource) to be a python2 unicode/python3 str, as it goes though this [method](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/utils.py#L300). But on django-storages side, every path/filename goes though the internal method ```_encode_name```, which converts to bytes in python2 (with ```smart_str```, an alias for ```smart_bytes``` in python2). Hence my ```UnicodeDecodeError```.

I'll open a PR soon. Any feedback welcome.

issue_title: "fill_date" in datetimes_to_durations
issue_body:
Hello,

According to the documentation, `fill_date` in utils.datetimes_to_durations can also be used to censor `end_dates` that are above `fill_date`:
> fill_date: the date to use if end_times is a None or empty string. This corresponds to last date
                  of observation. Anything above this date is also censored. Default: datetime.today()

It is currently not implemented. Here's the current computation of `C` (L613)
`C = ~(pd.isnull(end_times).values | (end_times == "") | (end_times == na_values))`

Also, the warning at L624 should be modified. At the moment, it prints *Warning: some values of start_times are __before__ end_times* in case where `(T < 0).sum()`. As `T` contains the result of `end_dates - start_dates`, *before* should be replaced by *after*.

issue_title: How does Customer_lifetime_value work?
issue_body:
Hi, I'm having trouble understanding the GammaGammaFitter function **customer_lifetime_value**.

In the [Lifetimes documentation](http://lifetimes.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Quickstart.html#estimating-customer-lifetime-value-using-the-gamma-gamma-model), the CLV of customer_id 1 is calculated to 1      140.096211 for time = 12 and discount_rate=0.01 used in the documentation.

Given that the conditional average expected profit is 24.658619, doesn't that assume that customer 1 will make ~140/25 = 5.6 purchases in the 12 coming months? Given that the customer made 2 repeat purchases in the calibration period, doesn't 5.6 seem too high?

Shouldn't CLV simply be equal to expected number of transaction in holdout period * conditional average expected profit, if ignoring the discount rate?

issue_title: Button Updates

This sample code will update the button text in realtime based on what the user enters into the input field:

```
import PySimpleGUI as g

read_button = g.ReadFormButton('Read', bind_return_key=True)
layout = [[g.T('Form doesn\'t close')],
          [g.T('New Button Text'), g.In(key='text')],

form = g.FlexForm('Persistent form with button updates')
form.Layout(layout)

while True:
    button, values = form.ReadNonBlocking()
    if button is None and values is None:

    read_button.Update(values['text'])

issue_title: `enabled=False` doesn't work for methods that access `response['id']`
issue_body:
Currently python-mailchimp contains code like this in various methods:

```python
class ListMembers(BaseApi):
    def create(self, list_id, data):
        # ...
        response = self._mc_client._post(url=self._build_path(list_id, 'members'), data=data)
        self.subscriber_hash = response['id']
        return response
```

Unfortunately, when `enabled=False`, this fails because `self._mc_client._post` returns `None`:
```
>       self.subscriber_hash = response['id']
E       TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
```

issue_title: Redis data needs to be decoded to string for Python 3
issue_body:
On Python 3.4 (Django 1.7) admin view /admin/defender/blocks throws TypeError "'str' does not support the buffer interface".

This is because with Python3 redis .keys() returns a bytes list while utils.strip_keys() expects a string list. I have a simple fix on my fork https://github.com/nephridium/django-defender/tree/decode_redis_data. If you like I can do a PR?

issue_title: Preserving the most recent backups
issue_body:
Let's assume that I have two backups:

```
backup-2016-01-10_21-15-00.tar.gz
backup-2016-01-10_21-45-00.tar.gz
```
Then I do:

```
$ rotate-backups --hourly=1 --dry-run dir/ 
2016-08-03 12:44:41 tonyhost rotate_backups.cli[6556] INFO Performing a dry run (because of --dry-run option) ..
2016-08-03 12:44:41 tonyhost rotate_backups[6556] INFO Scanning /home/tonyo/tmp/backup_rotate/dir for backups ..
2016-08-03 12:44:41 tonyhost rotate_backups[6556] INFO Found 2 timestamped backups in /home/tonyo/tmp/backup_rotate/dir.
2016-08-03 12:44:41 tonyhost rotate_backups[6556] INFO Preserving ~/tmp/backup_rotate/dir/backup-2016-01-10_21-15-00.tar.gz (matches 'hourly' retention period) ..
2016-08-03 12:44:41 tonyhost rotate_backups[6556] INFO Deleting ~/tmp/backup_rotate/dir/backup-2016-01-10_21-45-00.tar.gz ..
```

So `rotate-backups` removes the second, more recent backup (..._21-45-...). Could you please clarify why we don't want to keep the most recent one, and remove the one made at 21:15?
Does it make sense to add some option (like `--keep-recent`) to change this behaviour and keep the latest backups?

As a general (and somehow related) question, is it planned to introduce a `minutely` option to enable rotation if backups are made several times per hour?

issue_title: Is possible emit a binary file from python?
Improve object cleanup when connection fails
issue_body:
When wrapping a socket connection in a try, so an exception can be caught if the connection can't be made because the socket server is unreachable, e.g.
try:
    ws = SocketIO('localhost', 8080, wait_for_connection=False)
except:
    handle_exception()

An uncaught exception occurs: 
Exception ignored in: bound method SocketIO.__del__ of <socketIO_client.SocketIO object at 0x7f9be435efd0

This is because errors raised by __del__ output to stderr and are not caught.
The cause of this looks to be in _close() when halt() is called on _heartbeat_thread This is because there is no _heartbeat_thread for the object as an exception occurred during initialization. 

A suggested solution is to wrap the halt() call in a try block, e.g.
        try:
            self._heartbeat_thread.halt()
        except:
            pass

I haven't made a pull request with this as the solution as the author may not want this behavior, so I wanted to check first


<bountysource-plugin>
---
Want to back this issue? **[Post a bounty on it!](https://www.bountysource.com/issues/16376356-improve-object-cleanup-when-connection-fails?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F388415&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github)** We accept bounties via [Bountysource](https://www.bountysource.com/?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F388415&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github).

issue_title: Filters not implemented
issue_body:
Hi, thanks for your work on making a jsonpath library for Python.

I think this library might have a bug or are missing this part of the spec:

> using the symbol '@' for the current object. Filter expressions are supported via the syntax ?(<boolean expr>) as in
> 
> $.store.book[?(@.price < 10)].title
>
> ...
> 
> $..book[?(@.isbn)] | filter all books with isbn number
> $..book[?(@.price<10)] | filter all books cheapier than 10

I'm trying to filter an array by a string. My query, `$.phoneNumbers[?(@.type=='home')].number,  works fine on [the jsonpath.com testing tool](http://jsonpath.com/), but I can't get it working using jsonpath-ng. It fails to parse the query, complaining about the question mark.

Example code:

```python
import json
from jsonpath_ng import jsonpath, parse

data = '''
{
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName" : "doe",
  "age"      : 26,
  "address"  : {
    "streetAddress": "naist street",
    "city"         : "Nara",
    "postalCode"   : "630-0192"
  },
  "phoneNumbers": [
    {
      "type"  : "iPhone",
      "number": "0123-4567-8888"
    },
    {
      "type"  : "home",
      "number": "0123-4567-8910"
    }
  ]
}
'''
data = json.loads(data)
jsonpath_expr = parse(data)
jsonpath_query = "$.phoneNumbers[?(@.type=='home')].number"
```

Exception: `JsonPathLexerError: Error on line 1, col 15: Unexpected character: ?`

I'm trying this with jsonpath-ng>=1.4.3

Any ideas? Am I doing something wrong?

issue_title: README example does not emit records
issue_body:
README provides quick example of usage:

    import logging

    import json_log_formatter

    formatter = json_log_formatter.JSONFormatter()

    json_handler = logging.FileHandler(filename='/var/log/my-log.json')
    json_handler.setFormatter(formatter)

    logger = logging.getLogger('my_json')
    logger.addHandler(json_handler)

    logger.info('Sign up', extra={'referral_code': '52d6ce'})

Testing that on Debian Jessie 64-bit with Python 2.7.9 and using `JSON-log-formatter==0.0.2` there
is no output in the log file.

The reason is simple, default log level is WARN and example creates INFO log entries.

There are two options, either set log level to INFO: `logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)`  or to create log records by
    `logger.warn('Sign up', extra={'referral_code': '52d6ce'})`

issue_title: Some clients send a payload with the UPGRADE packet
issue_body:
The UPGRADE packet can sometimes come with a payload. The BestHTTP Socket.IO client sends it as a `52` instead of just a `5`. See https://github.com/miguelgrinberg/Flask-SocketIO/issues/175 for details.

The engineio upgrade should allow these payloads and still carry out the upgrade.

issue_title: Enhance parsing and representation of fields
issue_body:
Right now we have representers and parsers in order to deal with serialization and deserialization from the database in pydal. 
The fact we have a single `Field` class with a type stored inside the field lead us to check for that type every time among the whole code-base, in order to find the correct representation or parsing methods. This can be avoided if we silently create Field subclasses for every field type we have in pydal. Here is the idea:

1) The current syntax and user-side logic is preserved: it will works and see just the `Field` class
2) pydal should create dynamically (or we can define them statically) the `Field` subclasses for the types eg. `IntegerField`, `StringField`, etc.
3) Once a new `Field` is created with a type, pydal will actually store it as an instance of the correct subclass
4) every subclass is responsible of the whole logic regarding its type, so, for example, instead of finding the correct parser for a field type, the parser will directly invoke the class `parse` method

With this we can also bind expression to the correct field types, eg. the `sum` or `avg` method have no sense to exists on a text field.

@niphlod @mdipierro comments?

issue_title: [RoadMap] Legacy issue resolution before 1.0 release
issue_body:
We are working on multiple new features and refactors (gluon, sparse, engine, etc) towards an 1.0 release. But there are also some legacy issues that needs to be resolved. Here is a list of issues I have noted. Feel free to raise new issues or contribute fixes.

# Basic
- Sort out int32, int64, index_t, mx_uint etc.
Currently TShape uses int64_t but the front-end and back-end interface still use uint32_t for indices.
This need cleaning up and int64_t interface need to be exposed through a new set of CAPI. The general policy going forward should be to use int64_t for indices/size and int32_t for number of dimensions. Signed int should be used for function arguments unless there is a really strong reason to use unsigned.
- Deprecate mshadow.
Remove usage of mshadow and replace with Kernel::Launch or hand written cpu/gpu kernels.
- Verify type and shape support for operators.
Currently some operators don't support types other than fp32 for legacy reasons. Proper type support and/or documentation should be added.
Currently some operators have limit support for tensor ranks (maximum 5 dims). The limit needs to be increased or removed if possible.
- move legacy operators to new nnvm registration.
Conv, FC, BN etc should be refactored into stateless operators and use thread_local to store cudnn tensor descriptors.
- remove mkl experimental and use the new storage type interface.

# Stretch goals
- Support pybind11 or cython interface for faster python API.

issue_title: A question about shared weights in RNN
issue_body:
Hi, 
I have a question about how weights are shared in RNN unrolled. I notice that since the weights in every timestamp are the same, so these weights are represented by the same variable in the rnn example. My question is when we do back propagation, how the gradients are computed? Since the gradient variables correspond to the arguments, I only get one gradient variable for each weight. So does it mean this gradient is calculated by taking weights in every time stamp in consideration and I only need to update the weight once using this gradient?

issue_title: [Discussion] CreateBackwardOp interface for `Operator`
mx.metric.Perplexity should support calculating from cross-entropy
issue_body:
Currently part of the logic for calculating cross-entropy loss is in mx.metric.Perplexity. This means we would have repeated calculation when we are already using cross entropy loss, which is very commonly used (e.g SoftmaxCrossEntropyLoss). In that case, only exponentiating is needed.

https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/metric.py#L648-L658

issue_title: Bug of CuDNN RNN with variable sequence length
issue_body:
## Description
Segfault will be triggered by the following code:

```python
from mxnet.gluon.rnn import LSTM
import mxnet as mx
import numpy as np

ctx = mx.gpu()
lstm = LSTM(num_layers=1, hidden_size=200, dropout=0.5)
lstm.initialize(ctx=ctx)
batch_size = 32
for seq_len in range(500, 10, -1):
    for repeat in range(10):
        print(seq_len, repeat)
        inputs_nd = mx.nd.random.normal(0, 1, shape=(seq_len, batch_size, 200), ctx=ctx)
        out = lstm(inputs_nd)
        print(out[0].sum().asscalar())
        mx.nd.waitall()
```

I'm using V100 + cuda 9.0 + cudnn 7.0.4 (P3 instance).  The GPU memory keeps increasing and finally raises seg fault.

Also, the same script + configuration has not triggered an error in M60 (g3 instance).

@eric-haibin-lin @DickJC123 @szha @szhengac

issue_title: Detect invalid falsy event handlers
issue_body:
As of jQuery 3.0, event handlers that are falsy but not `false` now throw an error at some point in their lifecycle; in the past if the argument was falsy it would be a chainable no-op. Check for this case, warn about it, and return it to being a chainable no-op. Might as well also warn for crazy things like strings or numbers there, it really needs to be a function.

Ref https://github.com/jquery/jquery/issues/2812 (reverted for 1.12.0/2.2.0), https://github.com/jquery/jquery/pull/2249, https://github.com/jquery/jquery/pull/2248

issue_title: Put the file name in the warning message so I don't need to look at the stack trace
issue_body:
The name of the file where the code triggering the warning is should be part of the warning message, either at the beginning or at the end so it can easily be spotted.

Quoting dmethvin from another ticket:

> To keep the message readable and short I think it should just be the file name part, not the full URL. The docs in warnings.md should also be updated to indicate the file name is in the message.

Use case: sometimes you have warnings (elt's consider the case of warning about deprecated stuff that still works) due to third party libraries that you're never going to fix, so they make a lot of noise that you can't get rid of, while you are trying to spot the migration issues in your own code.

It's painful to have to inspect the traces one by one.

        self.handler = handler or self._construct_sock_handler()
        self.logger = logging.getLogger()
        self.logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
        if not self.logger.handlers:
            self.logger.addHandler(self.handler)

        log_sock.connect("tcp://127.0.0.1:%i" % self.port)
        time.sleep(0.1)
        handler = PUBHandler(log_sock)
        return handler


    def get_logger(self):

issue_title: keyserver should warn if no gpgkey available
issue_body:
If the user has gpg-agent (true with modern gpg) running, but the needed gpg key has not been loaded into the agent, any sops decryption will fail.

The sops error messages just indicate gpg failure, but not a failure to receive a key. The keyserver output reports as "decryption failed", rather than a "key not available" error. E.g.:

    [PGP]    INFO[0206] Decryption failed                             fingerprint=xxx

I believe a "key not available" message would be more helpful in pointing the user to the likelihood they haven't added their gpg key locally.

issue_title: Restructure SOPS to be class based
issue_body:
The current implementation of SOPS is functionally based. This is good for programs that rely on transforming data. However, it's beginning to get unwieldy with all of the state passing it is doing (e.g. `digest`, `iv`, `key`, and in #47 `unencrypted_suffix`).

I suggest considering a refactor to swap out the core with something more class based so state is always located on `self`. I will create a gist to provide examples of what I mean.

issue_title: Measure the WER increase vs comutation time decrease with a BiRNN stride of 2
issue_body:
Normally a RNN has time stride of `1` in time
```python
stride = 1
outputs = []
state = initial_state
with tf.variable_scope("RNN"):
  for time_step in range(0, num_steps, stride):
    if time_step > 0: tf.get_variable_scope().reuse_variables()
    (cell_output, state) = cell(inputs[:, time_step, :], state)
    outputs.append(cell_output)
```
However, a stride of `2` in time requires half the computation
```python
stride = 2
outputs = []
state = initial_state
with tf.variable_scope("RNN"):
  for time_step in range(0, num_steps, stride):
    if time_step > 0: tf.get_variable_scope().reuse_variables()
    (cell_output, state) = cell(inputs[:, time_step, :], state)
    outputs.append(cell_output)
```
Doing so with both RNN's of the BiRNN should cut computation by about a factor of two. However, it will also increase the WER.

The question to answer here is if this WER increase is worth the cut computation time.

issue_title: Remove date limits imposed by Python's datetime module to support BC and far-future dates
issue_body:
We currently rely on Python's built-in `datetime` module – and its `datetime` and `date` constructs – to represent and return date/time information, but this module only supports dates between 1 and 9999 AD. The in-built Python libraries for handling date/times are simply not designed or intended for representing dates in the distant past such as any BC dates, or dates in the far future.

Currently we ignore "extreme" dates that cannot be handled by `datetime` by bounding recognised dates to the `datetime.MINYEAR` (1) and `datetime.MAXYEAR` (9999) [boundary constants](https://docs.python.org/2/library/datetime.html).

We will update the date parsing and processing in *python-edtf* to avoid this limitation by removing any use of the `datetime` module which imposes the limits, and will use Python's much more flexible [struct_time](https://docs.python.org/3/library/time.html#time.struct_time) representation when returning values that were previously returned as `date` or `datetime` objects.

While this change will make it more awkward to work with some of the API, it will allow us to better represent the real data (rather than bounding the supported date ranges) and should be relatively easy for users to convert into richer Python objects like `datetime`s when possible (1 AD <= year <= 9999 AD) or something like [astropy.time](http://docs.astropy.org/en/stable/time/index.html) for dates outside `datetime`'s supported range.

**WARNING**: This change will cause a breaking API change for the following methods of EDTF classes which will return `struct_time` constructs instead of `date` or `datetime` objects:
* lower_strict()
* upper_strict()
* lower_fuzzy()
* upper_fuzzy()
* or indeed any features that rely on the underlying `_strict_date()` methods

issue_title: Non-ASCII searches fail because python-oauth2 is broken
issue_body:
Searching for a non-ASCII term causes this error:

    >>> import discogs_client
    >>> c = discogs_client.Client('user_agent', 'bogus_key', 'bogus_secret')
    >>> c.search(u'caf\xe9'.encode('utf8')).page(1)
    Traceback (most recent call last):
    [ ... snip ... ]
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/oauth2/__init__.py", line 442, in to_url
        urllib.urlencode(query, True), fragment)
      File "/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.8_2/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/urllib.py", line 1357, in urlencode
        l.append(k + '=' + quote_plus(str(elt)))
    UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters in position 3-4: ordinal not in range(128)

This is due to a known bug in python-oauth2: https://github.com/simplegeo/python-oauth2/issues/154. That library, unfortunately, appears to be abandoned: [the last commit](https://github.com/simplegeo/python-oauth2/commit/a83f4a297336b631e75cba102910c19231518159) was in 2011 (c.f. [this lament](http://pydanny.com/the-sorry-state-of-python-oauth-providers.html) over the sorry state of Python OAuth libraries).

It's worth noting that this was not an issue when sending non-ASCII requests *without* OAuth authentication because they don't go through the broken library. This has only come up recently because the search API now requires authentication, so we're now these previously-working requests are now hitting this bug.

As unattractive as it seems, I think the only solution may be to move to a more modern OAuth library. Personally, I've had success with [rauth](https://github.com/litl/rauth).

import numpy as np

issue_title: TimeDeltaParameter can't be passed to dynamic dependencies
issue_body:
I have a task with a TimeDeltaParameter, let's call it TaskA. I can run TaskA from the command line like so
```
luigi --module project.modules TaskA --buffer '1 minute'
```
TaskA has a dynamic dependency, TaskB, which I yield from the run function like so
```
def run():
    yield TaskB(self.buffer)
```
This results in the error
```
luigi.parameter.ParameterException: Invalid time delta - could not parse 0:01:00
```
It seems like passing TimeDeltaParameters to dynamic dependencies results in this issue, because they are passed as strings and then can't be parsed. Even more interesting, is when requiring TaskB from the requires function, it works just fine. Digging deeper, it looks like the parse function which throws the above error, isn't called when running dependencies from the requires function. Does anyone know how to get around this? I'm also curious as to if there's any way to set a default TimeDeltaParameter of 0 days as that seems to throw the same error as above.

issue_title: Regarding kinesis list_streams `next token` invention
issue_body:
Hi,

So far I found various botocore clients return `NextToken, nextToken, NextMarker, Marker, NextContinuationToken, position,  whatNot` etc. marker as key-value in the root level of response if there are more items to fetch. However, [Kinesis list_steams](http://botocore.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/kinesis.html#Kinesis.Client.list_streams) breaks this pattern. It wants next token to be the last item from the list of steams. Seriously! You guys are breaking your own invention. Why don't you follow similar pattern as other clients?

I have a utility function that automatically loops through tokens and fetches items based on various markers. Now Kinesis list_streams() breaks this totally. I would greatly appreciate if this behavior is modified as other clients.

Thanks //Zia

issue_title: six dependency

issue_title: Easiest way of adjusting the scaling factor for short strings in process.extract?
issue_body:
The docstring says:

According to the docsting process.extract undertakes the following steps 

#. Run full_process from utils on both strings
#. Short circuit if this makes either string empty
#. Take the ratio of the two processed strings (fuzz.ratio)
#. Run checks to compare the length of the strings
    * If one of the strings is more than 1.5 times as long as the other
      use partial_ratio comparisons - scale partial results by 0.9
      (this makes sure only full results can return 100)
    * If one of the strings is over 8 times as long as the other
      instead scale by 0.6

What is the easiest way of adding and additional length scaledown? Like if its 2 or 3 times as long scale by 0.8 or 0.7?

PS.: Love The algo

The `TableElementFinder` is poorly designed and it needs refactoring. The `TableElementFinder` does not support all the same locator strategies which are supported by the rest of library. It would be better to redesign the whole class to be better unified with the rest of the library. Improvements for `TableElementFinder` - [ ] Add more acceptance tests - [ ] It should not matter which locator strategy is used to identify the table where actions are performed - [ ] Support also webelements - [ ] When identifying the table, support also identifying table by element above, example if DOM looks like this `
...
` then ` | Table Should Contain | id=table | item |` should work - [ ] Desing is is possible to return table content to Robot side.

issue_title: `Get Cookies` should support returning cookies as objects and have better logging
issue_body:
Currently `Get Cookies` returns a cookies as a string in format `cookie1=value1; cookie2=value2`. This isn't very useful if you need to actually handle all cookies somehow, and the return format isn't even documented anywhere. We should change the keyword to return cookies as a list (or ordered dictionary) of CookieInformation objects that were introduced as part of #932.

Changing keyword return value is obviously backwards incompatible, so we cannot do it without a deprecation period. I propose that in v3.1 we add an argument like `return_objects=False` that can be used to explicitly change the return value. This allows users to opt-in if they want to get objects. In v3.2 we could then change the argument to `return_objects=True`, making it possible to opt-out if needed. And in v3.3 or later the whole argument can be removed. This issue only covers adding `return_objects=False` (or similar), changing and removing it later require separate issues later.

Another problem in `Get Cookies` is that it doesn't log anything. The beginning of the return value is logged automatically if the return value is assigned to a variable, but it would be better to log at least names and values explicitly.

---

**UPDATE:** @Mohorelien commented below that there actually is a use case for the current format in HTTP request headers. I still think having a way to return cookie information as objects is useful, but we should preserver the current functionality too. I'd also keep the current functionality as the default functionality to avoid all backwards incompatible changes. That way this change could even be done in SL 3.0 if we have time.

When I add some blog posts I always get errors... This time it's this error:

```

Request Method: 	GET
Request URL: 	http://localhost:8000/de/
Django Version: 	1.8.7

'NoneType' object has no attribute 'paginate_by'

Exception Location: 	/venv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/djangocms_blog/views.py in get_paginate_by, line 66
```

### OS:

`Fedora:23`

### Details about local setup:

It's [this project](https://github.com/fau-fablab/website/tree/djangocms_blog). If you want you can inspect all files and even run the docker container...

- django `1.8.7`
- djangocms `3.2.0`
- djangocms blog `0.3.6`
- packages from pypi
- python 3
- virtual environment

### Steps to reproduce:

It's hard to describe as I have done this steps some times and everything an other error occurred.

- I installed djangocms as described in the docs (I think)
- I moved the blog to the top in the admins view so that it appears under `http://mysite.com/` (**maybe this causes the problem**)
- I added two blog posts in admin view
- I navigated to `/` and got the error
